revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image: a game-development pixel-art animated sprite sheet.

CANVAS AND SHEET CONTRACT:
- Exact image size: 1024x512 px.
- 2 rows x 4 columns, 8 frames total.
- Each frame cell is exactly 256x256 px.
- Arrangement: TOP row = frames 1,2,3,4 left to right. BOTTOM row = frames 5,6,7,8 left to right.
- Do NOT draw grid lines, cell separator lines, frame borders, frame numbers, labels, text, logos, watermarks, UI, or any markings.
- Background must be a flat, uniform, solid #FF00FF chroma-key magenta in all empty space, no gradients, no texture, no shadow blobs.
- Strict containment: every frame stays fully inside its 256x256 cell with clear solid magenta margin on all four sides. No body part, scabbard piece, strand, cord, paper streamer, or pixel fragment crosses a cell boundary.
- No detached fragments: no isolated pixel islands, no dust specks, no leftover effect residue, no floating pieces.

STYLE:
- Crisp high-resolution 16-bit-plus pixel art, SNES-inspired but larger and more detailed.
- Pixel clusters must be readable and sharp, no antialiasing blur, no painterly softness.
- Consistent palette across all frames.
- Dark one-pixel outline where useful.
- Top-left light source.
- Bright cheerful mythical-Japan action RPG palette.

REFERENCE CHARACTER LOCK:
Use the attached reference image as the exact character identity and pixel-density reference. Treat this as re-posing the same character, not designing a new one. Match the approved reference character’s proportions, head-to-body ratio, body width, outline style, palette, and pixel density exactly.
- Character: Mito, androgynous 15-year-old wanderer.
- Proportions: about 2.4 heads tall, sturdy grounded build, solid chest, thick limbs, broad stable stance. NOT slim, NOT lanky, NOT taller than the reference.
- Every frame keeps exactly the same head size, total standing height, sturdy body width, limb thickness, and scale.
- Draw the character large in each cell, filling most of the cell height with a small clear magenta margin.
- Same character identity in all 8 frames. Re-pose only; do not restyle, resize, or redraw with different proportions.

VIEW AND DIRECTION:
- This is an 8-frame walk cycle facing UP in a top-down 3/4 RPG view.
- Back view: show the back of the head, black short hair, tiny low ponytail at the nape with vermilion cord, and the back of the indigo haori.
- No face is visible. Do not show eyes or front facial features.

OUTFIT DETAILS TO PRESERVE:
- Ink-black short hair tied into a tiny low ponytail at the nape.
- Vermilion braided-cord hair ornament.
- White tube-sleeved top.
- Short indigo-blue haori jacket, seen mostly from the back, with small vermilion braided-cord knots on sleeves and hem.
- Loose young-leaf-green hakama trousers tucked into dark leggings with red cord ties.
- Light travel sandals.
- Vermilion braided cord belt-sash with small tassel.
- Necklace with three pale-green magatama beads may be subtly hinted only if visible from the back/side angle, but do not turn the character toward the viewer.
- At the left hip hangs a sheathed short ritual sword in a WHITE scabbard, wrapped with a thin sacred rope and tiny white paper streamers. It reads as a festival ritual implement, not a samurai katana.

SWORD RULE:
- The white scabbard stays sheathed at Mito’s LEFT hip across the lower back in EVERY frame.
- The scabbard keeps the SAME steady position, length, angle, and scale in every frame.
- It must not drift side to side, rotate, change length, or be held in the hand.
- No drawn sword blade; no unsheathing.

WALK CYCLE REALISM:
Make a believable 8-frame walk cycle for ONE direction only, facing up/back view. Use proper alternating footsteps. Feet stay on the same ground line in every cell, with slight body-height changes as specified. No frame may look floating, tip-toeing, or sliding.

Frame poses:
1. CONTACT A: Mito’s LEFT foot planted forward/upward in the travel direction, right foot trailing/back, arms counter-swing.
2. WEIGHT DROP: same step transition, body 2-3 pixels lower, planted foot flat under weight.
3. PASSING: feet together under the body, body at mid height.
4. PUSH-OFF: body 2-3 pixels higher, push-off readable.
5. CONTACT B: Mito’s RIGHT foot planted forward/upward, left foot trailing/back, mirror step of frame 1.
6. WEIGHT DROP: same opposite step transition, body 2-3 pixels lower.
7. PASSING: feet together under the body, body at mid height.
8. PUSH-OFF: body 2-3 pixels higher and loops smoothly back to frame 1.

Motion requirements:
- Left foot leads in frame 1, right foot leads in frame 5.
- Never repeat the same leading leg in the contact poses.
- Arms swing opposite to legs in every frame; arms and legs change pose together.
- The lifted soles read clearly from the back view.
- Haori back panel and sash knot sway subtly with each step.
- Leg silhouettes must visibly differ between consecutive frames; do not copy the same leg pose across frames.
- Keep stride length consistent between left-foot and right-foot contact poses.
- Head height is consistent for upright frames except the specified 2-3 pixel body bob; do not resize the head.

NEGATIVE CONSTRAINTS:
- No Mario, Nintendo, Zelda, Final Fantasy, Pokemon, Dragon Quest, Secret of Mana, Okami, Sakuna characters, logos, likenesses, or emblems.
- No samurai armor, kabuto helmet, ninja outfit, two-sword daisho, gun, modern objects, horror mood, dark gloomy palette, bird, companion creature, letter emblems, modern text, watermark, UI.
- No grid lines or text.

Output must be one clean 1024x512 PNG sprite sheet only.
